and when himself give up the ghost , recommend his soul into the hand of his father , will not leave our soul in hell , nor will he suffer his holy one to see corruption . though the shell of our body be break , the precious kernel of our soul shall not be lose : these earthen pot may crack , but the jewel in they shall be preserve : there lie a hide Mannah within ( not our golden , but ) our earthen pot , which be not capable of worm and corruption . let that proud insult conqueror , who ride upon the pale horse , bruise the satchel of our body ( as the tyrant do that of Anacharsis ) unto dust , yet over our soul , which be our self , he have no power . be not dismay ; though our mistress , nature , strip we of the garment of our body , as Potiphars wife do Joseph , yet of our ▪ soul she can rob we : she give we the garment , it be her own , she may challenge it ; but the soul be no gift of she , she have no title to it , she can claim it . disease , infirmity , and injury , like so many sodomite , may beset these house of our body ; but they can injure our soul , which be the angel lodge within we . the celestial fire of our soul shall never be extinguish , though the temple of our body in which they burn shall be destroy . that fire which consume the temple of peace at roam , do not hurt to the Palladium that be in it ; neither shall the conflagration of our body in a calenture or burning-fever , prejudice or hurt our soul . the vestal virgin be not more careful to rescue the Palladium from the flame , than the good angel , our minister spirit , shall be to convey our soul out of these flame , unto a place of refresh . therefore my soul shall not be dismay , though she be carry in this weak and leak ship of a infirm body , on the wave of the red sea of persecution ; for even from hence she smell by faith the sweet odour of her heavenly Arabia , though as yet with her bodily eye she can see it . the hot fiery furnace of affliction shall no more consume and annoy she , than the flame do consume the fiery bush , or the fiery furnace of Babylon do the three child . the Presteres live in the fire , and be not burn ; fresh water spring out of the salt sea , and yet be not thereby infect ; nor be the fish salt which live in salt water : neither shall our soul either suffer by sickness in the body , or die with the body ; but after she have fight the good fight , like a conqueror or emperor , she shall be carry out of this campus Martius upon the shoulder , not of senator , but of angel . and as a eagle fly out of the funeral pile when it be set on fire , leave the body of the emperor to be consume ; so shall our soul fly up unto their maker , leave their body to be waste by time and corruption . for , as it be impossible for the body to die till the soul forsake it , which be the life of it ; so much more impossible be it for the soul to die , until God , who be her life , forsake she : and that will never be , till God himself cease to be ; for he have promise never to forsake we , his love like himself be unchangeable . a mother may forget the fruit of her womb , father and mother may , and will forsake we , but the lord will never forget or forsake we ; but when friend , and all leave we , he will then receive we : therefore let our soul magnify the lord , and let our spirit rejoice in God our saviour . finis .

sect . vi . 1. then let we consider , whether the worship of saint and angel be more for the glory of God , than to pay all religious worship to God alone . now if religious worship be for the glory of God , than all religious worship be more for God glory than a part of it ; unless man will venture to say , that a part be as great as the whole . and yet whoever worship saint and angel , though he be neve so devout a worshipper of God also , yet he give part of religious worship to creature , and therefore god can have the whole , unless they can divide their worship between God and creature , and yet give the whole to God . if it be object , that those who worship saint and angel , do not give that worship to they , which be peculiar and appropriate to the supreme God , and therefore they reserve that worship which be due to God , whole to himself , though they pay a inferior degree of religious worship to saint and angel . i answer , what that worship be , which be peculiar to the supreme God , i shall consider more hereafter ; but for the present , suppose that they give only a inferior degree of worship to creature , be this religious worship , or be it not ? if it be , be a degree of worship a part of worship ? if it be , than God have not the whole , and therefore be not so much honour , as if he have the whole ; as to show this in a plain instance . those who pray to saint and angel , though they do not pray to they , as to the supreme God , but as to mediator and intercessor for they with the supreme God , yet they place a inferior degree of hope and trust and affiance in they , or else it be nonsense to pray to they at all ; so that though God may be the supreme object of their reliance and hope , yet he be not the only object ; he have part , and the great part , but not the whole , for they divide their hope and trust between God and creature ; and if it be a great glory to God to trust whole in he , than to trust in he in part , than it be a great glory to God to pray to he only , than to pray also to saint and angel . nay it be more than probable , that those who pray to saint and angel , as trust in their merit and intercession for they , do not make God , but these saint and angel , to who they pray , the supreme object of their hope . this it may be will be think a extravagant charge against man who profess to believe , that God be the supreme lord of the world , and the sole giver of all good thing ; but this be no argument to i , but that notwithstanding this belief , they may trust more in saint and angel , than in God , and consequent give the supreme worship to they . for man do not always trust most in those who have the great power , but in those by who interest and intercession , they hope to obtain their desire of the sovereign power . thus i be sure it be in the court of earthly prince ; though man know , that the king only have power to grant what they desire , yet they place more confidence in a powerful favourite than in their prince , and when they have obtain their request , pay more solemn acknowledgement to their patron ; for let the power be where it will , our hope and trust be place there where our expectation be . and when man expectation be not from the prince , who have the power , but from the favourite , who interest direct the influence of this power to they , which otherwise will never have reach they , such favourite have more numerous dependent , more frequent address , more formal courtship , than the prince himself . and when man model the heavenly court accord to the pattern of earthly court , and expect the conveyance of the divine blessing to they as much from the intercession of saint and angel , as they do to obtain their desire of their prince , by the mediation of some powerful favourite , no wonder , if they love , and honour , and fear , reverence and adore , trust and depend on saint and angel , as much or more than they do on the supreme God . for there be not a more natural notion , than to honour those for our god , from who hand we receive all good thing , whether we receive it from their own inherent power or not , deus nobis haec otia fecit , namque erit ille mihi semper deus , illius aram saepe tener nostris ab ovilibus imbuet agnus . man may acknowledge God to be the supreme be , and ascribe incommunicable perfection to he , and yet may pray more frequent , more devout , more ardent , with great trust and affiance to saint and angel , than to God , as it be apparent many devotoe of the virgin mary do ; and this be to give supreme and sovereign worship to they , without acknowledge they to be supreme being . indeed it be moral impossible , but our religious worship , trust and affiance , must be at least equal share between the supreme God and our mediator , whatever he be , as man do not less trust in the interest of their patron , than in the power of their prince ; for it be not mere power but favour , which be the immediate object of our trust ; and therefore God appoint his only beget son to be our mediator , as for other great and wise reason , so to prevent idolatry by give we a god incarnate , who be a proper object of religious adoration , to be our mediator , that see man will worship their mediator , they may have a god for their mediator to worship . the sum be this : if it be more for the glory of God to have all religious worship appropriate to himself , than to have only a part of it , and it may be the least share and part too , than the worship of saint and angel can be for god glory . but beside this , the worship of saint and angel , together with God , do mighty obscure and lessen the divine perfection , and therefore it can be for his glory . it represent he indeed like a great temporal monarch , but it do not represent he like a God . that which we ignorant think a piece of state and greatness in earthly monarch , to administer the great affair of their kingdom , to receive petition and address , to bestow favour , to administer justice by other hand , to have some great minister and favourite to interpose between they and their subject , be nothing else , but want of power to do otherwise . he will be a much great prince , more belove and reverence , who can do all this himself ; but no prince can be present in all part of his kingdom , nor know every particular subject , much less their particular case , and condition , desert and merit ; and therefore be force to divide this care into many hand , and in so do share his power and honour with his subject . but whoever imagine any such thing of God , deny his omnipresence , his omnipotence , his omniscience , and his particular care and providence over his creature . God indeed do not always govern the world by a immediate power , but make use both of the ministry of angel and man ; but he govern all thing by his immediate direction , or at least by his immediate inspection . he overlook every thing himself , while all creature either obey his command , or submit to his power . if this be the true notion of god govern the world , that he have the concernment of the whole creation under his eye , and keep the disposal of all thing in his own hand , so that nothing can be do , but either by his order or permission ; then the most perfect and glorious angel , the great minister of the divine providence , can challenge no share in religious worship , can be the object of our trust or hope , because they be only minister of the divine will , can do nothing from themselves , as civil minister of state , and officer of great trust can in temporal kingdom , but be always under the eye , and always move at the command of God . in such a state of thing all the peculiar right of sovereign power and dominion God reserve whole to himself , as any wife prince will , among which the receive the prayer and petition of his creature be none of the least ; to hear prayer be make the peculiar attribute of God in scripture , thou be a god that hear , prayer , therefore unto thou shall all flesh come . and reason tell we , that it be the most eminent part of sovereignty and majesty ; and the reason why temporal prince do not reserve this whole to themselves , be because they can do it ; but God can , and he challenge it to himself , and will not allow any creature to do it ; and there be no temptation to pray to any creature , when we know , that they can help we , that they must receive their order and command from God , and not act by their own will and inclination . thus prince have their eavourite , to who they express a very partial fondness and respect , to who they will deny nothing that they ask , nor hardly show any grace or favour to their subject without they ; and this force subject to address themselves to their prince by they ; but it be a reproach to the divine goodness and universal providence , to conceive any such thing of God ; which yet be the foundation of the worship of patron saint and angel , as person so dear to God , that he can deny their request , and will not grant our petition without they , or at least , that it be the most certain and effectual way to obtain what we desire , to offer up our prayer and petition to God by their hand . no doubt but all good man on earth , much more bless saint and angel in heaven , as be more perfect and excellent creature , be very dear to God ; but yet God be not fond and partial in his kindness , as earthly prince be , but have a equal regard to all his creature , and delight in do good to they , and need not to be importune by any powerful favourite to hear their cry and prayer ; he will as soon attend to the prayer of a humble penitent sinner , as of the most glorious saint , and be more ready to grant than they be to ask . a mediator of redemption be very consistent with all the perfection of the divine nature , and do mighty recommend both the goodness and wisdom of God to the world . when mank have transgress the law of their creation , they forfeit their natural right and interest in the care and goodness of their maker ; the divine justice , and the wisdom of God in the government of the world , require a atonement and expiation for sin ; and it be a amaze demonstration of the divine goodness to sinner , that he find one himself , that he give his son to be a propitiation for our sin . when man by sin have forfeit their original innocence and happiness together , they can expect nothing from God , but by way of covenant and promise ; and every covenant between contend party , must be transact in the hand of a medaitor , and none so fit to be our mediator , as he who be our ransom too . and a mediator must be invest with power and authority to see the term of this covenant perform , and this be his mediatory intercession . he intercede not mere as a powerful favourite , but as the author and surety of the covenant , not mere by entreaty and prayer , but in virtue of his blood , which seal the covenant , and make atonement and expiation for sin . thus Christ be our mediator of redemption , who have redeem we by his blood ; and we must offer up all our prayer to God in his name and powerful intercession , because we can expect no blessing from God , but by virtue of that covenant , which he purchase and seal with his blood . but now a mediator of pure intercession , without regard to any atonement make for sin , or any covenant of redemption ( such as saint and angel and the bless virgin be make by the church of roam ) be a mighty reproach to the divine nature and perfection . it clothes god with the passion and infirmity of earthly prince ; represent he as extreme fond of some of his creature , and very regardless of other ; as if his kindness to some favourite saint , be a more powerful motive to he to do good , than his own love to goodness ; as if he know not when , nor to who , to show mercy without their direction or counsel , or will not do it without their importunity ; as if some of his creature have as much the ascendant over he , as some favourite have over their prince , who can with a word speak have any thing of they , and extort favour from they , even against their will and inclination . no man can think there be any need of such intercessor and mediator with God , who believe he to be infinite wise , and to be infinite good ; to know when it be fit to hear and to answer , and to be always ready to do , what his own wisdom judge fit to be do . there can be no place for such intercession and entreaty , to a infinite perfect be ; for they always suppose some great weakness or defect in he who want they , for even a wise and a good man want no mediator to persuade he to do that good , which be fit to be do . the objection against this be very obvious , and the answer , i think , be as easy . the objection be this : if God be so good , that he need not such prayer and intercession to move he to do good , why do we pray for our self ? why do we pray for one another ? why do we desire the prayer of good man here on earth ? why be it a great reproach to the divine perfection to beg the prayer of saint Paul , or saint Peter , now they be in heaven , than to have beg their prayer , while they have be on earth ? to this i answer : when we pray for our self , i suppose , we do not pray as mediator , but as supplicant , and nothing can be more reasonable , than that those , who want mercy , or any other blessing shall ask for it . it be certain no reproach to the divine goodness , that God make prayer the condition of our receive , which be a very easy condition , and very necessary to maintain a constant sense and reverence of God , and a constant dependence on he . and when we pray for one another on earth , we be as mere supplicant , as when we pray for our self , and to pray as supplicant be a very different thing from pray as advocate , as mediator , as patron . the virtue of the first consist only in the power and efficacy of prayer ; the second in the favour and interest of the person . This the church of roam her self own , when she allow no mediator and advocate , but saint in heaven , which be a sign , she make a vast difference between the prayer of saint on earth , and saint in heaven . there be great and wise reason , why God shall command and encourage our mutual prayer for each other while we be on earth ; for this be the noble exercise of universal love and charity , which be a necessary qualification to render our prayer acceptable to God ; this preserve the unity of the body of Christ , which require a sympathy and fellow-feeling of each other suffering , this be the foundation of public worship when we meet together to pray with , and for each other to our common father ; and it give a great reputation to virtue and religion in this world , when God hear the prayer of good man for the wicked , and remove or divert those judgment which they be afraid of ; this become the wisdom of God , and be no blemish to his goodness , to dispense his mercy and favour in such a manner as may best serve the great end of religion in this world . God do not command we to pray for our self or other , because he want our importunity and solicitation to do good , but because it serve the public end of religion and government , and be that natural homage and worship , which creature owe to their great creator and benefactor , and sovereign lord . but to imagine , that God need Advocates and mediator to solicit our cause for we in the court of heaven , where none of these end can be serve by it , this be a plain impeachment of his wisdom and goodness , as if he want great importunity to do good , and be more move by a partial kindness and respect to some powerful favourite , than by the care of his creature , or his love to goodness . Erom hence it evident appear , how inconsequent that reason be , from our beg the prayer of good man on earth , to prove the lawfulness of our pray to the saint in heaven to pray and intercede for we ; the first make they our fellow supplicant , the second make they our mediator and intercessor ; and how little the church of roam gain by that distinction , between a mediator of redemption , and mediator of pure intercession ▪ for though they pray to saint and angel only as mediator of intercession , yet this be a real reproach to the nature and government of God ; a mediator of redemption be very consistent with the divine glory and perfection , a mediator of pure intercession be not . and the sum of all be this , that it be so far from advance the divine glory to worship saint and angel together with God , that it be a real reproach and dishonour to he ; and therefore this can be no law nor institution of our saviour , who come not to abrogate the divine law , but to fulfil and perfect they . some think there be no danger of dishonour God by that honour they give to saint and angel , because they honour they as God friend and favourite , as those who God have honour and advance to great glory ; and therefore whatever honour they do to they , rebound back again on God , and this may be true , while we give no honour to saint and angel , but what be consistent with the divine glory ; but when the very nature of that honour and worship we pay to they , be a diminution of God glory , and a reproach to his infinite perfection , as i have make it appear , the worship of saint and angel be , sure it can be for god glory to advance his creature by lessen himself .

CHAP. Iii . concern the heavenly part of the christian life , which be the proximate mean of obtain heaven ; show what virtue it consist of , and how much every virtue contribute to the happiness of heaven . virtue in the general , consist in a suitable behaviour to the state and capacity in which we be place ; now man , who be the subject of that virtue we be here discourse of , be to be consider under a threefold capacity . the first be of a rational animal ; the second of a rational animal relate to God ; the three of a rational animal relate to all other creature . and these be the only capacity of virtue that be in humane nature : so that all the virtue we be oblige to , and capable of , consist in behave our self suitab to the state and condition of rational animal , that be relate to God and their fellow creature . by which three capacity of our nature , the virtue or suitableness of behaviour which we stand oblige to , be distinguish into three kind , viz. the humane , the divine , and the Social . humane virtue consist in behave our self suitab to the state and capacity of mere rational animal : divine virtue consist in behave our self suitab to the condition of rational animal relate to God : Social virtue consist in behave our self suitab to the capacity of rational animal relate to their fellow creature , but especial to rational creature that be of the same class and society with we . that i may therefore proceed more distinct in this argument , i shall endeavour to show what those virtue of the christian life be , which be proper to a man in each of these capacity ; and how much each of those virtue contribute to the happiness of heaven . sect . i . concern those humane virtue which belong to a man as he be a reasonable animal , show that they be all include in the heavenly part of the christian life , and that the practice of they effectual conduce to our future happiness . first we will consider man in the capacity of a mere rational animal , that be compound of contrary principle , viz. spirit and matter , or a rational soul and humane body ; by which composition he be , as it be , the buckle of both world , in who the spiritual and material world be clasp and unite together ; and partake , as he do , of both extreme of spirit and matter , angel and Brutus , there arise within he from those contrary nature contrary propension , viz. rational and sensual , or angelical and brutish ; and in the due subordination of these , his sensual to his rational propension , consist all humane virtue . for his reason be the noble principle of his nature , must be suppose to be implant in he by God to rule and govern he , to be a eye to his blind and brutish affection , to correct the error of his imagination , to bind the extravagancy of his appetite , and regulate the whole course of his action ; so as that he may do nothing that be destructive or injurious to this excellent frame and structure of his nature . but now in this compound nature of a man , there be his concupiscible and irascible affection : with the first of which he desire and pursue his pleasure ; and with the second , he shun and avoid his danger ; and there be also bodily appetite , such as hunger , thirst , and carnal concupiscence ; and together with these a self-esteem and valuation ; all which be the natural subject of his reason , and indeed the only subject upon which it be to exercise its dominion . so that in the well and ill government of these , consist all humane virtue and vice . to the perfect well govern therefore of a man self , there be five thing indispensable necessary : 1. that he shall impartial consult his reason what be absolute best for he , and by what mean it be best attainable , and and then constant pursue what it propose and direct he to . for so far as he be want in this , he cast off the government of his reason . 2. that he shall proportion his concupiscible affection to the just value which his reason set upon those thing which he affect : for every degree of affection which exceed the merit of thing , be irrational , and consequent injurious to our rational nature . 3. that he shall not suffer his irascible affection to exceed those evil and danger which he will avoid ; for if he do , they will prove great evil to he than those evil or danger be which raise and provoke they . 4. that he shall not indulge his bodily appetite to the hurt and prejudice of his rational nature : for if he do , he will violate the noble , for the sake of the vile part of himself . and 5. that upon the whole , he shall maintain a modest opinion of himself ; and not think better of his own conduct and management of himself than it deserve : for by so do , he will be apt to overlook his own misgovernment , and so incapacitate himself for any far improvement . and in these five particular consist all that virtue which belong to a man , consider mere in the capacity of a rational animal : the first be the virtue of prudence , the second be the virtue of moderation , the three be the virtue of fortitude , the four be the virtue of temperance , the five be the virtue of humility . all which , as i shall show , be essential part of the christian life , and such as do effectual contribute to our heavenly happiness . I. prudence . and this be the root and groundwork of all other virtue ; it|be this that give law and scope to all our motion , that propose the end , and prescribe the measure of our action . for prudence consist in be guide and direct by right reason , as it propose to we the worthy end , and direct we to the fit and most effectual mean of obtain they . so that to live prudent , be to live in the constant exercise of our reason , and to be continual pursue such end as right reason propose , by such mean as right reason direct we to , which be the proper business of all the virtue of religion . and hence religion in the scripture be frequent call by the name of wisdom or prudence ; the fear of the lord that be wisdom , say job , and to depart from evil that be understanding , Job xxviii . 28 . And , the fear of the lord be the beginning of wisdom , say David , Psal. cxi . 10 . where the fear of the lord comprehend all the act of religion , which be therefore wise and prudent , because they be the fit mean to those worthy end which right reason propose . so that to exercise our reason in the search and discovery of what be absolute best for we , and to follow our reason in the pursuit and acqu of what it discover to be so , be that virtue of prudence whereunto we stand oblige as we be rational animal . for our reason be the noble principle of our nature , that by which we be raise above the level of brute , yea , by which we be ally to angel , and do border upon God himself , ought upon that account to be submit to , as the supreme regent and directress of all our other power and to be look upon as the rule of our will , and the guide of all our animal motion . and when to gratify our sensual appetite , or unreasonable passion we either neglect those end which our reason propose to we , or pursue they by such mean as our reason disallow of , we reverse the very order of our nature , and tread antipode to our self ; and while we do so , it be impossible we shall be happy , either here , or hereafter . for every thing , you see , be disease while it be in a unnatural state and condition , while its part be displace , or put into a disorder , or distort into a unnatural figure . and so it be with a man , who while he preserve his faculty in their natural station and subordination to each other , while he keep his affection and appetite in subjection to his will , and his will to his reason , he be calm and quiet , and enjoy within himself perpetual ease and tranquillity ; but when once he break this order , and suffer his passion or his appetite to usurp the place of his reason ; to impose contrary end to it , or prescribe contrary mean ; his faculty , like disjoint member , be in perpetual anguish and anxiety . and hence it be that in the course of a wicked life , we feel such restless contention between our spirit and flesh , between the law in our mind , and the law in our member ; because our nature be out of tune , and its faculty be displace and disorder , and that sovereign principle of reason which shall sway and govern we , be depose , and make a vassal to our appetite and passion . for in all our evil course our choose and refuse , resolve and act , not as reason direct we , but as sense and passion biass we ; and our reason have nothing to do in all this brutish scene of action , either sleep it out , without mind or regard , or else sit by as a idle spectator of it , and only censure and condemn it . and it be this that cause all that tumult and contest that be in our nature ; and till , by the exercise of prudence , our faculty be reduce , and set in order again , our mind will be like our body , while its bone be out of joint , continual restless and unquiet . and therefore to remove this great indisposition of our nature to happiness , prudence be require of we , as one of the principal virtue of the heavenly part of the christian life . for thus our saviour enjoin that we shall be wise as serpent as well as harmless as dove , Mat ten . 16 . which though it be here prescribe in a particular case only , viz. that of persecution ; yet since the reason of it extend to all other case , and it be fit we shall be prudent in all our undertake as well as in suffer persecution , it be upon that account equivalent to a universal command . so also Ephes. v. 15 . see that you walk circumspect , not as fool , but as wise ; i . e . in the whole course of your action take heed that you follow the guidance of your reason , and do not suffer your self to be seduce by your blind passion and appetite , which be mere ignes Fatui , or the guide of fool . and accord then apostle pray for his Christian colossian , that they may be fill with the knowledge of God in all wisdom and spiritual understanding , Coloss. i . 9 . i . e . that they may have such a knowledge of god will as may render they true prudent , and cause they to pursue the best end by the best mean . and though this virtue seldom occur in the new testament under its own name , yet , as in the abovenamed place it be express by wisdom , so it be elsewhere by knowledge , as particular , 2 Cor. vi . 6 . where he command the minister of the church to approve themselves such by several virtue , and particular by pureness , i . e . continence , and by knowledge , i . e . by prudence ; for , beside that knowledge as it signify a understanding of divine thing , be not a virtue in the apostle , but a gift of God , and so not proper to be enumerate among these virtue ; there be hardly any account to be give why the apostle shall place knowledge in the midst of so many moral virtue , if he do not thereby mean the virtue of prudence , which be , as it be , the eye and guide of all the other virtue . so again , 2 Pet. i . 6 . where he bid we add to faith , virtue , i . e . fortitude , or constancy of mind ; and to virtue knowledge , and to knowledge temperance ; by knowledge it be high probable he mean prudence , because he place it in the midst of those two virtue which border near upon prudence . now that the practice of this virtue be a most proper and effectual mean of our everlasting happiness , be evident from hence , because the practice of it be a constant exercise of reason . for to act prudent in religion be to follow the best reason ; to aim at heaven , which be the best end , and direct our action thither by the best rule ; it|be to consult what be best for our self , and how it may be most effectual obtain . in a word , it be to intend the chief good above all , and to level our life and action most direct towards it . this be religious prudence in the general ;
